stories.title: Microsoft and LinkedIn Just Analyzed the Future of Work and AI. It All Points to One Key Skillset
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft and LinkedIn studied how AI will change work in the future. They found that one particular skill is becoming more important than others.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're learning new skills or training staff, it might be worth paying attention to what skill they're recommending — though the headline doesn't say what it is.

stories.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is closing its Mechanical Turk service to new customers. This was a platform where people could sign up to do small tasks online for money.
stories.why_it_matters: If you relied on Mechanical Turk as a income source, you won't be able to join anymore, but existing customers can still use it for now.

stories.title: Forget Micron? This New AI Memory Stock Could Be the Best Buy of the Decade
stories.simple_summary: An investment article is recommending you buy stock in a company that makes AI memory chips, saying it could be a great investment.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
stories.simple_summary: Google made a funny (or annoying, depending on your taste) commercial showing what would happen if the Founding Fathers had used Google's AI tools to write the Declaration of Independence.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a commercial — it doesn't affect your daily life or business.

stories.title: Philosophers Are the Latest Hiring Target for AI Companies
stories.simple_summary: AI companies are starting to hire philosophers — people who study big questions about right and wrong, knowledge, and how things work.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI companies are thinking about serious questions like ethics and safety, but it doesn't directly change anything for you.

stories.title: Alibaba bans Anthropic's Claude Code after an alleged hidden China-detection backdoor is uncovered — employees told to switch to Qoder as the rift between the firms widens
stories.simple_summary: Alibaba (a Chinese company) stopped letting employees use Claude Code, an AI writing tool made by another company. Alibaba says it's too risky.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you work at Alibaba, this doesn't affect you — it's a business disagreement between two tech companies.

stories.title: Palo Alto Networks and Koi Security sued over alleged AI error in cyber threat report
stories.simple_summary: A company called Palo Alto Networks is being sued because their AI made a mistake when reporting on a cybersecurity threat, and the wrong information may have caused harm.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows AI isn't perfect at everything — if you rely on AI security tools, remember they can make mistakes, just like people can.

stories.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
stories.simple_summary: Google made a commercial joking about what it would be like if the Founding Fathers had used Google's AI tools to help write the Declaration of Independence.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a commercial — it doesn't affect your daily life or business.

stories.title: AI security questions loom over NATO summit
stories.simple_summary: Military and government leaders from NATO countries are discussing concerns about AI security at a big meeting.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly affects government policy — nothing you need to act on personally.

stories.title: The Artificial Intelligence (AI) Memory Supercycle Is Getting Stronger. Here's How You Can Profit From This Boom With Less Than $100
stories.simple_summary: An investment article says AI memory chips are becoming more valuable and suggests you could invest in them with a small amount of money.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
stories.simple_summary: Midjourney (a company that makes AI art) is in a legal fight with movie studios and is asking the studios to admit how they're using AI in their films.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal dispute between big companies — nothing you need to act on personally.

stories.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
stories.simple_summary: Alibaba told its employees they cannot use Claude Code, which is an AI tool made by another company, because the company thinks it's risky.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you work at Alibaba, this doesn't affect you — it's a business decision by one company.
